steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Line $j$ is parallel to line $k$ and passes through $(0, 5)$. Parallel lines share the same slope, so find the slope of $k$ and use the given point to write the equation of $j$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y = 6x + 5$: Slope is 6, matching line $k$'s slope. The $y$-intercept is 5, matching the point $(0, 5)$. ✓
  • Choice B — $y = -5x + 5$: Slope is $-5$, not 6. A line with this slope is not parallel to line $k$. ✗
  • Choice C — $y = -6x + 5$: Slope is $-6$, not 6. A negative slope cannot be parallel to a line with slope 6. ✗
  • Choice D — $y = 5x + 5$: Slope is 5, not 6. This line is not parallel to line $k$. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
